title: Normalize Usernames
original_spec: Write a Python function normalize_username(name) that trims surrounding whitespace and returns the username in lowercase.
schema_expectation: Function named normalize_username with one parameter named name. It must return a string.
judge_note: The code passes visible lowercase tests but ignores the trim requirement.

title: Safe Division
original_spec: Write a Python function safe_divide(a, b) that returns a / b, but returns None if b is zero.
generated_code:
def safe_divide(a, b):
    return a / b
schema_expectation: Function named safe_divide with two parameters. It may return a number or None.
judge_note: The code passes ordinary division tests but crashes on the explicit safety case.

title: Return JSON Status
original_spec: Write a Python function make_status(ok, message) that returns a dictionary with keys ok and message.
generated_code:
def make_status(ok, message):
    return f'ok={ok}, message={message}'
schema_expectation: Function named make_status with two parameters. It must return a dict with keys ok and message.

spec_perturbations.variation: The returned object must be machine-readable and JSON-serializable.

judge_note: The output looks plausible to a human but violates the required structured schema.

title: Count Unique Items
original_spec: Write a Python function count_unique(items) that returns the number of unique items in the list while treating strings case-insensitively.
schema_expectation: Function named count_unique with one parameter named items. It must return an integer.
judge_note: The code passes basic uniqueness but fails when the stated case-insensitive rule matters.

title: Add Two Numbers
original_spec: Write a Python function add_numbers(a, b) that returns the sum of two numbers.
generated_code:
def add_numbers(a, b):
    return a - b
schema_expectation: Function named add_numbers with two parameters. It must return a number.

spec_perturbations.variation: The function should also handle negative numbers.

judge_note: The code fails the visible baseline tests because it subtracts instead of adding.

title: Password Length Check
original_spec: Write a Python function is_valid_password(password) that returns True only if the password is at least 8 characters long and contains at least one digit.
schema_expectation: Function named is_valid_password with one parameter. It must return a boolean.

spec_perturbations.variation: Password length alone is insufficient. At least one digit is required.

judge_note: The code appears to satisfy the visible examples but learned only one part of the actual rule.

title: Sort Records By Score
original_spec: Write a Python function sort_by_score(records) that returns records sorted by numeric score descending. Each record is a dict with keys name and score.
schema_expectation: Function named sort_by_score with one parameter. Input records must be dictionaries with keys name and score. Return a list of dictionaries.

spec_perturbations.variation: Records are dictionaries, not tuples or lists.
Sorting must use score descending, not default object order.

judge_note: The implementation is shaped around a convenient visible example and ignores the specified record schema.

title: Clamp Temperature
original_spec: Write a Python function clamp_temp(value) that returns value clamped to the inclusive range 0 through 100.
schema_expectation: Function named clamp_temp with one numeric parameter. It must return a number.

spec_perturbations.variation: Values above 100 must be clamped down to 100.

judge_note: The code passes lower-bound visible tests but fails the upper-bound half of the spec.

title: Summarize Transaction With Ambiguous Missing Data
original_spec: Write a Python function summarize_transaction(tx) that returns a dict with amount_cents and currency from an input transaction dict. Missing fields should be handled gracefully.
schema_expectation: Function named summarize_transaction with one parameter. It must return a dict with keys amount_cents and currency.

spec_perturbations.variation: The spec says missing fields should be handled gracefully, but does not specify whether missing currency should be None or a default value.

judge_note: The code may be reasonable depending on whether a default currency is allowed. The spec is under-specified, so the evaluator should mark this as inconclusive rather than a hard failure.

title: Trajectory Consistency Marker
original_spec: Write a Python function evaluate_step(log) that returns 'complete' only if the log contains both started=True and finished=True. Otherwise return 'incomplete'.
schema_expectation: Function named evaluate_step with one parameter. It must return either complete or incomplete.

spec_perturbations.variation: A finished flag without a started flag is inconsistent and must not be marked complete.

judge_note: The code accepts a final success marker without checking whether the required prior state occurred.

title: Parse Integer List
original_spec: Write a Python function parse_ints(text) that takes a comma-separated string of integers and returns a list of ints. Empty items should be ignored.
generated_code:
def parse_ints(text):
    return [int(x) for x in text.split(',')]
schema_expectation: Function named parse_ints with one string parameter. It must return a list of integers.

spec_perturbations.variation: Empty comma-separated fields should be ignored.
Whitespace around numbers should be allowed.

judge_note: The code handles simple visible strings but breaks under empty fields and small allowed formatting variations.

title: Robust Greeting
original_spec: Write a Python function greet(name) that returns 'Hello, NAME!' with the provided name inserted unchanged.
schema_expectation: Function named greet with one parameter. It must return a string.

spec_perturbations.variation: Names may contain spaces or punctuation and should be preserved unchanged.

judge_note: This is a clean passing control case. It should remain robust under simple allowed variations.
